content.full_text:
BRUNICK, ALVAREZ & BATTERSBY
PEIOFESSIONAL LA\^/ CORPORATION
I839 COMMERCENTER \]v EST
POST OFFTCE BOX 5425
sAN BERNARDINO, CALIFORNIA 92412
IELEPHONE: AREA COOE 7IZ}
aa9-930r a2:r-o623
2I5 CAJON STREE'T
P O. BOX t320
REDLANDS, CALIFORN IA 92373
TELEPHON E \7t41 793-Oet A
PLEASE TEFER IO
7MW-0015
August 4, 1992
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, Sulte 8OO
P. O. Box 8O7
Sacramento, CA 958O4-O8O7
AL''' 7'w
Re:
Request for Advice pursuant to Government Code Sectj.on
83r.r.4( b )
To the Commi"ssion:
On behalf of Mojave Water Agency Board of Director members John
Russell and George Parker, I am requesting advice regarding each of
their duties and responsibilities under the conflict-of- interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act. The facts which we believe
are pertinent to this request are as follows:
FACTS
John Russell and George Parker are each members of the Board of
Directors of Mojave Water Agency ( "t'I!{A" ). 
Director RusselL has
served contj.nuously in this capacity since 1982, after serving an
appointed term from approximately 1970 to 1978. Director parker
was elected to the Board of Directors in November 199L.
I,WA is located in the County of San Eernardino, and is a public
agency organlzed and existing under Chapter 2146 of Statutes of
L959, known as the Moj ave Water Agency Law of L959 of the State of
California (the "Act"). 
Pursuant to the Act, MWA is authorized to
take such actions as are necessary to ensure that sufficient water
may be available for the present or future beneficial- use or uses
of the lands and inhabitants located within MWA. MWA'S por.rer
includes the right to appropriate water, and to acquire and protect
rights for any beneficial purpose, and the right to store,
regulate, control, transport, divert and distribute water for use
within MWA.
In this regard, MWA is statutorily 
authorized to 
commence,
maintain, defend in and compromise any action in the State courts,
involving the use, ownership or supply of water, water rights, or


Fair Political Practlces Commission
August 4, L992
Page -2-
water service within MWA as well as outside of MWA, where such
water, water rights, or water service is or may be used, or is
useful, for any purpose vrithin MWA, or involves or affects the
interference or dlminution of water flow used or useful for any
purpose within MWA. By statute, MWA is authorized to adJudlcate
all rights to extract vrater Affecting water supply within its
boundaries and to determine the natural safe yield of ground water
basins and stream systems affecting the water supply within the
MWA.
In May 199O, a complaint for declaratory and injunctive relief, 
and
a petition for writ of mandamus, was filed in the San Bernardino
County Superior Court entit.Led Cit\,r of Barstoi^, and the Southern
California water Company vs. City of Adelanto, et a1 . (Case No.
BCV6672). There r"rere approximately L50 naned defendants, including
MWA. By their action, the plaintiffs, 
who use water from the
Moj ave River System, sought to have the court determine the amounts
of water from the Mojave River System that the Clty of Barstow and
Southern California Water Company are entitled to have fl-owing to
them through the Barstow Gauging Station. The plaintiffs 
alleged
that the Moj ave River System is in a state of overdraft due to
cumulative r.rater production by each defendant and upstream of
Barstow, and that the defendants' actions are thereby impairing the
water supply to the plaintiffs.l
In June, 199L, MwA filed a cross-complaint entitled Moiave Water
Aoencv v. Anderson, et aI ., in the above action. A flrst 
amended
cross-compl-aint was then filed in JuIy J,991. In addition to all
parties lncluded in the Barstow complaint, MWA's cross-complaint
named all parties which would be significantly affected by the
relief 
sought in the Barstow complaint, including parties
downstream of Barstot"r, who were not named as plaintiffs 
or
clefendarrts in the complaint ( including individuals, cities, county
and/or county entities, water districts, 
and private parties ) . The
named cross-defendants include those who pump at feast s-acre feet
each year.2 The cross-defendants include Board of Director Parker-
At the time the cross-complaint was filed, Dlrector Parker had not
yet been elected to the Board of Directors of MWA.
lOverdraft is a condition whereby more water is being
taken from the ground than is bej.ng replenished, and if
continued over a period of tj,me, can result in permanent
damage to the groundwater resource.
2An acre-foot is equal to 325,900 ga11ons, or the amount
of water used by a family of five in one year.


Fair Polltical
August 4, 1992
Page -3-
Practices Commission
The purpose of MWArs cross-complaint was and ls to obtaln an
adjudicatlon of lrater rights within MwA and the Mojawe Rlver area
of Lnfluence. The adjudication is necessary to ensure that the
water presently being taken fron the system exceeds the maxlmum
quantity of water which can be produced annually froin the avallable
supply without unreasonably depleting and causing the eventual
destruction of the ground water as a soutce of water supply for the
area.
In September 1991, I'rvJA filed a notion to stay the entlre actlon for
one year, or untll November l, 1992. That stay allows a standstill
on pleadings and prevents discovery (except limited discovery by
MWA) and summary judgment motlons. The stay allows one year for
the parties collectively to conplete engineering studles of the
water supply withln the MoJave River and its groundwater baslns.
The stay was ordered by the court on October 15, 1991.3
The effect of the MwAis cross-conplaint wlII be to adludicate aII
water rights to the Mojave River System, lncluding the rights of
any and all persons uho own property in thls area, and who ,row, or
in the future, wlll extract water for their personal or buslness
use. The adjudlcatlon would give an adjudicated vrater right to
each extractor ln the action; and each extractor lrou1d have to pay
for replacement water to recharge what they produce in excess of
the safe yield. 
The water rtght of a particular cross-defendant
may be dimlnished to a percentage of what they clain at this time;
the actual water rights wiII not be knordn or determlned until the
engineering studies have been completed.
The effect of a vote for adjudication 1s to vote for payment on a
net basis for any water extracted beyond the individual userrs base
percentage right of the total safe yield. 
The adiudication will
not financially benefit any pumper.
John Russell is not a defendant or cross-defendant in the pending
action. 
In addltion to being an elected member of the Board of
Dlrectors of MWA, he is also a nenber of the Board of Directors of
two nutual non-profit water companies. Director Russell serves as
a member of the Board of Directors of Center Water Company, located
1n Lucerne Valley. He also is on the Board of Directors of Jubilee
Mutual water Company, Iocated about 4 miles east of Lucerne vafley.
He and his wl-fe also own a share in the Bar-H Mutual Water Company,
Iocated five niles east of Lucerne VaIIey. 
Two of these water
3rn November
Riverslde County
1990, the entire case was transferred to
Superior Court, where It is presently.


Falr Political Practices Commisslon
August 4, 1992
Page -4-
companies have been naned as Roes Defendants to the MWArs cross-
complalnt.
Director and Mrs. RusseII's ownelshlp of the share ln Bar-H Mutual
Water Company is based upon their ownership of a residential lot
within the water company I s area. This water company covers two
streets, one nile long each. There are 48 parcels 1n the water
company, and each parcel has one vote. He and hLs wlfe have ovrned
the parcel for approxirnately one and one half years.
Center Water Company Is a small vrater companies that punps
approximately 1o-acre feet per year. Director RusseII has five
shares in Center Water Company. He has owned a duplex property
(worth around SIOO,OOO) and a vacant lot (i{orth approximately
S45,OOO) in the Center Water Company jurisdiction for trrenty years.
He ha6 al6o been an owner of the Lucerne Va1fey Shopping Center for
35 years. 
A general partnership (of rrhich he 1s a general
partner), owns appronimately one acre of the shopping center,
containing the parking lot and some buildings. A family trust owns
the other portion of the shopping center; he owns 1/3, and a
relative, 
Elma Ragensberg owns 2/3 in a trust. 
The partnership
leases the buildings to the shopping center. The book value of his
interest in the shopping center is more than S1,OOO, but less than
S1OO.00O. The fair market value of the shopping center property is
approximately 5400,000 (based on a bank appraisal done last year).
Jubilee llater Company pumps Just abowe the 1o-acre foot minimuln
each year. 
Each 1-114 parcel has one share in Jubilee Water
Company. There are a total of 516 shares j.n JublLee Water Company,
with 512 active shares. Director Russell owns one share in Jubilee
Water Conpany. which he has owned for twenty years. The shares
have approxlmately SlOO par value, although Director RusselI
believes they do not have negotiable value because they are
appurtenant to the property.
Dlrector RusseII and his wife also Iive on a 4o-acre parcel in
Lucerne Va11ey. They pump under lo-acre feet of water each year.
Director Russell holds the Bar-H property. the house and the
shopping center in joint tenancy with hls wife. 
He owns the other
propertLes outright.
It is antlcipated that the adJudlcatlon of water rights in the
cross-complaint wiII have little 
effect on the water rates paid by
the Shopping Center. because the use is a commercial use. However,
it is anticipated that the adjudicatlon may increase residential
vrater rates, although the anount of increase cannot be deternined


Fair Political Practlces Commission
August 4. 1992
at thls time.
George Parker is a cross-defendant in the pending Lawsuit.
Director Parker owns and lives on a 180-acre farm in the MWA area.
He farms between 20-25 acres at any one time. He grows horse hay.
and has one customer. His gross income has gone as high as S8,O0O
to S9,OOO per year. but ls currently approxlmatety $3,O00 to
S4,OOO. The income fron the hay business is less then 108 of his
and his wifers total annual income, and does not provide him with
any profit.
During one year, Director Parkerrs maximum pumplng amount is
appro:.imately 174 acre-feet. 
Last year he pumped 1O4 acre-feet.
There are three rde1ls on his property. 
As a result of the
adJudication, he w1II cut back on how tnany acres he farms, and
thus. how much water he uses.
Please let me know if this office can provide any additionaL
information, or be of further assi.stance.
Very truly yours,
BRUNICK, ALVAREZ & BATTERSBY
AG: nI
cc: John RusseII, Dlrector, MWA
George Parker. Director, MWA
Larry Rowe, General Manager, MWA

sections.question_synthetic: What are the conflict-of-interest duties and responsibilities of Mojave Water Agency Board members John Russell and George Parker under the Political Reform Act, given their various property interests and the pending water rights adjudication lawsuit that may affect their personal and business interests?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from counsel to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are formally marked, but the request structure is clear. Document contains detailed factual recitation supporting the advice request. No FPPC response/analysis present in this document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: What are the conflict-of-interest duties and responsibilities of Mojave Water Agency Board members John Russell and George Parker under the Political Reform Act, given their various property interests and the pending water rights adjudication lawsuit that may affect their personal and business interests?

embedding.summary: A law firm requests FPPC advice on behalf of two Mojave Water Agency Board members regarding their conflict-of-interest obligations under the Political Reform Act. The request details extensive facts about a pending water rights adjudication lawsuit and the directors' property holdings and financial interests that may be affected by MWA Board decisions.
